title: symphytum × uplandicum
intro: Symphytum × uplandicum, also known as comfrey, is a perennial plant that belongs to the borage family and is a hybrid of Symphytum officinale and Symphytum asperum. The medicinal properties of comfrey include its ability to heal wounds, reduce inflammation, and promote tissue repair due to its high levels of allantoin, a compound that stimulates cell growth and regeneration. The health benefits of comfrey include its use in treating bone fractures, sprains, and strains, as well as its ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce menstrual cramps. Comfrey is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being added to salves and creams to promote wound healing and skin health.
